mentions.note: Defensive pharma hedge recommended for recession scenario on credit cap policy risk.
Real wins from bountiful acquisition spree; CEO can spread wealth beyond current heavy Kruda exposure.

$70B commercial opportunity with 20+ growth drivers; diverse pipeline replacing Keytruda dependency; enlisticide pill form monoclonal, Winrevera expanding, new flu antiviral; line of sight to multi-year growth.
Old-fashioned solid earner without Tony Soprano overtones; strong runner alongside Johnson & Johnson and Amgen.
Keytruda-driven pharma with strong money flow; rallied 39% since October; broken above 50-day; 3% yield; recent 3–8% pullbacks ideal entry; acquired $6.7B cancer drug company today.
Down 1.3%; pharma sector acting worst when inflation risk rises; drugmakers vulnerable to stagflation.

Pharma stock; healthcare rotation underway. Cramer rates at 13x earnings as terrific but expects further pullback; suggests divide-by-10 mental trick (~$110 stock looks like $11 stock; could drop to $10.50).
Caller Mike (LA): Merck reported good quarter, fell on pharma weakness. CEO Rob Davis maneuvering well. Stock up $3 today. Cramer would 'press the button' at ~$120.
Watching cancer portfolio results Monday evening following ASCO conference; J&J challenging leadership, awaiting clarity on competitive position and outlook.
